summary: Conflicting headlines on U.S.-Iran negotiations have kept markets on edge, with Iranian state media denying Trump's claims of productive talks while Brent crude pushed above $104, leaving energy the only S&P 500 sector in positive territory for March.
tweet: Iran ceasefire doubts pushed crude above $104 while new Section 301 trade probes keep tariff uncertainty alive — two unresolved shocks squeezing stocks and bonds from both ends.
